data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow are muted as Fed official hints at a potential rate cut in July
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages are muted on Friday as Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller hinted at potential rate cuts in July.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finds itself near even, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) is up 0.2%, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has drifted lower by 0.1%.

Across the S&P 500, six of the 11 sectors are trading in the green, with Energy and Real Estate posting the strongest gains while Health Care has been the weakest performer so far.

U.S. Treasury yields have edged higher, with the shorter-end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) gaining 1 basis point to 3.94% while the longer-end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) is up by 3 basis point to 4.42%.

Today also marks the second triple witching event of the year—a day on which $6.5T worth of contracts tied to stock options, stock index futures and stock index options will expire. Such days have typically seen volatility in equity markets.

Waller said Friday he sees little risk that tariffs will stoke inflation and signaled the central bank could start cutting interest rates as soon as July.

On the geopolitical front President Donald Trump announced that he will decide within the next two weeks if the U.S. should launch an attack on Iran, pressuring Tehran to the negotiating table for a nuclear deal.

"Markets are recovering a bit this morning after White House spokeswoman Leavitt last night said that Trump had dictated a message saying that 'based on the fact that there's a substantial chance of negotiations that may or may not take place with Iran in the near future, I will make my decision whether or not to go within the next two weeks,'" Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

That means two weeks of uncertainty for financial markets, but investors are still inclined to see the Middle East conflict as a local, not a global, economic issue, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

From an economic vantage point, the Philly Fed Manufacturing gauge stalled at -4 in June, missing consensus. Furthermore, the U.S. Leading Indicator figures inched down 0.1% to 99.0 in May, matching the consensus estimates.

data.title: UK grocery regulator launches investigation into Amazon's payment practices
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) is being investigated by the UK’s top grocery regulator for failing to pay its suppliers on time, a breach of the country’s Groceries Supply Code of Practice and one that could result in a penalty of up to 1% of the retailer’s UK turnover.

The Groceries Code Adjudicator (“GCA”) opened a probe into Amazon’s (NASDAQ:AMZN) conduct towards its suppliers after warning the company last year to take “swift and comprehensive action” to comply with GCA code.

“Based on evidence [from monitored actions] it was clear that an investigation into compliance with paragraph 5 of the Groceries Code specifically was required.”

Article 5 in the UK Groceries Supply Code of Practice requires a retailer to pay a supplier for groceries delivered” within a reasonable time after the date of the supplier’s invoice.

Amazon’s (NASDAQ:AMZN) UK grocery business is relatively small compared to other markets, with a 1.6% to 3% market share versus 29% for UK grocery giant Tesco. Although Amazon (AMZN) does not separate net sales for its UK grocery business, the decision by the Competition and Markets Authority for Amazon (AMZN) to comply with the UK Grocery Supply Code of Conduct suggests its grocery business exceeds $1B turnover annually.

Amazon (AMZN) shares fell at Friday’s open and trading in the red for a third consecutive day.

data.title: Tandem's t:slim X2 insulin pump works with Abbott’s FreeStyle Libre
data.description:
• Tandem Diabetes Care (NASDAQ:TNDM) said on Friday that its t:slim X2 insulin pump now works with Abbott’s (NYSE:ABT) FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus glucose monitor.
  • The company has started an early access program in the U.S. and plans to expand availability in late 2025.
  • The FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus sensor can be worn for 15 days and sends glucose readings to the pump every minute, the company said.

data.title: Lucas GC announces pricing of follow-on offering; shares up nearly 5%
data.description:
• Lucas GC (NASDAQ:LGCL) announced on Friday the pricing of its “best efforts” follow-on offering of 32.15 million ordinary shares of the company at a public offering price of $0.20 per share, for total gross proceeds of $6.43 million before deducting placement agent's fee and offering expenses.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about June 23, 2025, said the company. 
  • AC Sunshine Securities LLC is acting as the placement agent for the offering.
  • LGCL is +4.72% to $0.7116.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. home-sale prices hit all-time high, but estimated to decline by 2025-end - Redfin
data.description:
Real estate brokerage Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) said on Friday that the median U.S. home-sale price hit an all-time high of $396,500 in the four weeks ended June 15, but is expected to decline by 2025-end.

The figure represents a year-over-year rise of 1%.

"Note that sale prices are seasonal and typically peak in June or July, and that price growth is cooling," noted the report.

"While the median home-sale price is sitting at a record high, it's notably lower than the median asking price. That's because sellers are open to negotiating in today's cooling housing market, in which sellers outnumber buyers," according to Redfin.

The median sale price is roughly $26,000 lower than the median asking price of $422,238.

The 6% discount marks a reversal from 2021 and 2022, when the market favored sellers and the median sale price was typically much higher than the median list price.

The current supply-demand scenario is likely to lead to a nationwide decline in home-sale prices by the end of the year, according to the real estate brokerage.

Related ETFs: iShares Residential and Multisector Real Estate ETF (REZ), SPDR S&P Homebuilders ETF (XHB), iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF (ITB), iShares MBS ETF (MBB), Invesco Building & Construction ETF (PKB), Direxion Daily Homebuilders & Supplies Bull 3X Shares ETF (NAIL), Vanguard Mortgage-Backed Securities Index Fund ETF Shares (VMBS), The Hoya Capital Housing ETF (HOMZ)

data.title: Apollo flags concerns over CPI data accuracy as estimates surge
data.description:
Apollo is raising red flags over the reliability of recent inflation data, noting a significant increase in the share of estimated inputs used in the Consumer Price Index calculation.

Each month, the Bureau of Labor Statistics compiles roughly 90,000 price quotes across 200 categories, with field agents collecting data from 75 urban areas. Typically, when data is unavailable, the BLS estimates around 10% of these values. However, in May, that figure jumped dramatically—nearly 30% of the data feeding into the CPI was estimated rather than directly observed.

“In other words, almost a third of the prices going into the CPI at the moment are guesses based on other data collections in the CPI,” said Torsten Sløk, Apollo’s chief economist.

This development follows softer-than-expected CPI inflation figures for May. Headline inflation rose just 0.1% month-over-month, falling short of the 0.2% consensus forecast. Core CPI, which strips out food and energy, also increased by only 0.1%, below the anticipated 0.3%.

The surge in data estimation raises questions about the precision of inflation readings at a time when policymakers and markets are closely watching for signs of cooling price pressures.

data.title: Top 10 overbought and oversold stocks on Wall Street
data.description:
After a strong rebound from its April 7 low, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has shown some signs of slowing momentum in June when compared to its strong performance in May with the index now wavering above and below the key 6,000 mark.

Now within 2.7% of its all-time high, the broader market’s rally has left a number of constituent stocks flashing technical warning signals. 

According to Seeking Alpha, a closer look at the Relative Strength Index, a widely used momentum indicator, reveals that several S&P 500 components have entered extreme territory. An RSI reading above 70 typically signals that a stock may be overbought, while a reading below 30 suggests it could be oversold. 

Currently, 11 stocks within the index are trading in overbought territory, while 17 have dipped into oversold conditions. See below the 10 most overbought and oversold stocks within the S&P 500:

Wall Street’s 10 Most Overbought Stocks

data.title: U.S. Leading Indicator slips 0.1% in May, as expected
data.description: The U.S. Leading Indicator inched down 0.1% to 99.0 in May, matching the consensus and a smaller decline than April's -1.4%, which was revised from -1.0%, according to data released by The Conference Board on Friday.

data.title: Kroger raises outlook for FY25 same store sales, but sees uncertain macroeconomic environment
data.description:
Led by solid pharmacy and e-commerce sales, as well as strong demand for its lower-priced private label brands, The Kroger Co (NYSE:KR) raised its outlook for FY25 same store sales, driving shares more than 7% higher at Friday’s open.

"Our strong sales results and positive momentum give us confidence to raise our identical sales without fuel guidance, to a new range of 2.25% to 3.25%. While first quarter sales and profitability exceeded our expectations, the macroeconomic environment remains uncertain and as a result other elements of our guidance remain unchanged,” Kroger CEO David Kennerley said in a statement.

The company’s updated same store sales guidance compares to prior guidance of +2% to +3%. Other metrics were left unchanged, with the grocery chain expecting an adjusted profit of $4.60 to $4.80 per share, straddling the consensus estimate of $4.76.

For the first quarter, Kroger (NYSE:KR) earned a profit of $1.49 per share, up 6 cents from a year ago and 3 cents better than expected. Adjusted FIFO operating profit increased by 1.3% to $1.52B, with a 79 basis point improvement in FIFO gross margin rate. Overall gross margin improved 100 basis points to 23.0%, attributed to Kroger (NYSE:KR) Specialty Pharmacy, lower shrink, and lower supply chain costs, partially offset by lower margin pharmacy sales.

Total sales were down less than 1% to $45.12B, which missed expectations by $190M. Excluding fuel sales, adjustment items, and sales in its Specialty Pharmacy category, net sales were up 3.7% year-over-year.

Kroger’s (KR) rally is contributing to strong gains for Albertsons Companies (ACI), Sprouts Farmers Market (SFM), Costco (COST), and Grocery Outlet (GO), as well as grocery suppliers that include United Natural Foods (UNFI), Sysco (SYY), and US Foods Holding (USFD).

data.title: J&J, AbbVie get EU endorsement for Imbruvica's expanded approval
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) said that an advisory committee of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed a label expansion for its and AbbVie's (NYSE:ABBV) cancer drug, Imbruvica.

The EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has issued a positive opinion recommending marketing authorization for an extended indication of Imbruvica to treat frontline mantle cell lymphoma.

This includes its use in combination with rituximab, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, and prednisolone, alternating with R-DHAP without ibrutinib, followed by ibrutinib monotherapy for adults with previously untreated mantle cell lymphoma eligible for autologous stem cell transplant.

The extended indication is based on data from a late stage trial of the drug, the company added.
